archetype: Automated Forensic DNA Profiling and Matching System for Mass Disaster Victim Identification
domain: Forensic Genetics and Mass Disaster Response
description: This system automates the process of DNA profiling and matching for mass disaster victim identification, used by forensic experts and disaster response teams. It integrates with various DNA analysis software and hardware, and must comply with strict quality control and data security standards. The system's complexity lies in handling large amounts of DNA data, ensuring accurate matching, and meeting regulatory requirements.
rules: MUST integrate with DNA analysis software such as STRmix and TrueAllele
MUST comply with ISO 17025 and ISO 27001 standards for forensic laboratories
MUST support multiple DNA profiling kits and instruments, including ABI and Illumina
MUST implement data encryption and access controls to ensure confidentiality and integrity
MUST meet the requirements of the National DNA Index System (NDIS) for DNA profile upload and search
anti_patterns: DO NOT use a generic data warehouse for storing DNA profiles, as it may compromise data security and compliance
DO NOT rely solely on manual data entry for DNA profile upload, as it may lead to errors and inefficiencies
DO NOT neglect to implement audit trails and access logs, as they are critical for regulatory compliance and data security
key_technical_challenges: Developing an efficient algorithm for DNA profile matching and search
Integrating with various DNA analysis software and hardware
Ensuring data security and compliance with regulatory standards

recommended_stack.language: Python + due to its extensive libraries for bioinformatics and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Flask + for building a scalable and secure web application
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL + for its support of advanced data types and indexing
recommended_stack.cache: Redis + for improving performance and reducing database queries
recommended_stack.message_broker: RabbitMQ + for handling asynchronous tasks and data processing
